nodes.name: Appointment Complete Trigger
nodes.notes: Receives POST request with patient data after appointment.

parameters.responseBody: ={ "status": "success", "message": "Review request workflow initiated", "patient": "{{ $('Validate & Structure Data').first().json.patient_name }}", "timestamp": "{{ $('Validate & Structure Data').first().json.timestamp }}" }

nodes.name: Webhook Response
nodes.notes: Returns 200 OK immediately. Fires before any wait nodes to prevent upstream timeouts.

nodes.name: Validate & Structure Data
nodes.notes: Validates incoming data. Outputs: patient_first_name, patient_email, clinic_name, clinic_manager_email, appointment_type, provider_name, appointment_notes, clinic_review_url, and supporting fields.

nodes.name: Lookup Opt-Out Sheet
nodes.notes: Looks up patient_email in the opt_out_log sheet. Always outputs data so workflow continues when email is not found. Sheet columns: patient_email | last_request_date | opted_out.

nodes.name: Dedup & Opt-Out Check
nodes.notes: Reads Google Sheets lookup result. Halts if patient opted out or was contacted within 90 days.

nodes.name: Wait 4 Hours Post-Appointment
nodes.notes:
TESTING: Set to .5 minute.
Production: Change amount to 4 and unit to hours.

options.system:
You write personalized post-appointment review request emails for dental clinics. Your tone is warm, brief, and direct. You write like a real person, not a marketer. Rules: - No em dashes - No semicolons - No rhetorical questions - No phrases like "we value your feedback" or "your opinion matters to us" - Active voice - Vary sentence length - Maximum 120 words in the body Return only valid JSON in this exact structure, with no preamble or markdown code fences: { "email_subject": "subject line here", "email_body": "HTML body with

tags and {{REVIEW_LINK}} placeholder where the link goes", "sentiment_flag": "positive" } The sentiment_flag should be "positive" unless the appointment notes contain clear indicators of a problem (complaint, pain, dissatisfaction, escalation), in which case set it to "negative".

nodes.name: Claude: Generate Review Email
nodes.notes:
Uses Claude to generate a personalized review request email. Returns JSON with email_subject, email_body (with {{REVIEW_LINK}} placeholder), and sentiment_flag.
TESTING: continueOnFail=true so Parse & Inject Review Link fallback handles Claude errors.

nodes.name: Parse & Inject Review Link
nodes.notes: Reads Claude output from json.content[0].text or json.text (whichever is present). Parses JSON, replaces {{REVIEW_LINK}} with hyperlink. On no output or JSON parse failure: returns fallback positive email — workflow continues. fallback_used field indicates which path was taken.

nodes.name: Sentiment Router
nodes.notes: Checks sentiment_flag field. TRUE (equals 'negative') → Alert Clinic Manager. FALSE → Send review request.

parameters.subject: =⚠️ Negative Patient Feedback - Action Required: {{ $('Validate & Structure Data').first().json.patient_name }}
parameters.html:
=

A patient may have had a negative experience.

Patient: {{ $('Validate & Structure Data').first().json.patient_name }}

Appointment type: {{ $('Validate & Structure Data').first().json.appointment_type }}

Provider: {{ $('Validate & Structure Data').first().json.provider_name }}

Notes: {{ $('Validate & Structure Data').first().json.appointment_notes }}

nodes.name: Alert Clinic Manager (Negative)
nodes.notes:
Fires when sentiment is negative. Review request suppressed.
SMTP: The authenticated SMTP account must be allowed to send from the configured fromEmail address.

nodes.name: Send Review Request Email
nodes.notes:
Sends personalized review request email. HTML body from Parse & Inject Review Link.
SMTP: The authenticated SMTP account must be allowed to send from the configured fromEmail address.

nodes.name: Log Send to Opt-Out Sheet
nodes.notes: Writes/updates patient row in opt_out_log sheet after each review request send. Uses patient_email as matching column for appendOrUpdate. Records last_request_date for the 90-day dedup window.

nodes.name: Wait 72 Hours for Response
nodes.notes:
TESTING: Set to .5minutes.
Production: Change amount to 72 and unit to hours.

nodes.name: Review Completion Check
nodes.notes:
TESTING: GBP review check bypassed. Always passes patient data through to follow-up.
Production: Restore full GBP review check logic and re-enable Fetch Recent GBP Reviews node.

parameters.subject: ={{ 'Just checking in, ' + $('Validate & Structure Data').first().json.patient_first_name }}
parameters.html:
=

Hi {{ $('Validate & Structure Data').first().json.patient_first_name }},

Just a quick follow-up from {{ $('Validate & Structure Data').first().json.clinic_name }}. We'd still love to hear about your recent visit.

If you have a moment, leave us a Google review here. It means a lot to our team.

Thanks again for choosing us.

nodes.name: Send Follow-Up (Day 3)
nodes.notes:
Day 3 follow-up. Static HTML with clinic name, patient name, and review URL.
SMTP: The authenticated SMTP account must be allowed to send from the configured fromEmail address.
